Reproduction / Scenario: Screen-reader announcement / role=status or aria-live
Expected Result: toast region exposes role=status or aria-live=polite
Actual Result: No role/aria-live in CSS or registry markup; pointer-events:none on region but no live region semantics
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS cannot add ARIA; component ships no semantic guidance
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add role=status aria-live=polite to .toast-region in registry markup/docs

Reproduction / Scenario: Positioning conflict: absolute CSS vs popover top-layer
Expected Result: Panel appears directly under its trigger
Actual Result: CSS positions content position:absolute relative to .navigation-menu-item, but showcase marks it popover which promotes to top layer where absolute offset is viewport-relative, breaking under-trigger anchoring
Root Cause Hypothesis: Popover elements render in the top layer; position:absolute resolves against the initial containing block, not the trigger
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Choose one model: drop popover and use :focus-within/:has, OR keep popover and use anchor positioning

Reproduction / Scenario: Keyboard focus shows on handle
Actual Result: Outline declared but handle is a plain <div> with no tabindex/role=separator/aria-valuenow in registry markup, so it is not focusable or announced
Root Cause Hypothesis: Handle needs tabindex=0 and role=separator with aria values; CSS focus style is dead without focusability
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document required attributes (tabindex=0 role=separator aria-orientation aria-valuenow)

Reproduction / Scenario: The project own showcase honors 0 lines of JavaScript
Actual Result: index.html contains 19 onclick, 5 showModal, 5 showPopover, 6 hidePopover inline handlers — JavaScript is required to demo the components
Root Cause Hypothesis: Modal/popover/toast components require JS to open or close; the headline 0 lines of JavaScript is contradicted by canonical usage
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Requalify headline to almost zero JS / no JS framework and note dialog/popover need one-liner triggers

Reproduction / Scenario: Check that npm package shadcss is published
Actual Result: registry.npmjs.org/shadcss returns HTTP 404 — package not published; npm install shadcss would fail
Root Cause Hypothesis: Library has never been published to npm; install/CDN instructions are aspirational
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Publish to npm, or mark install instructions coming soon / use a GitHub/jsDelivr URL

Reproduction / Scenario: unpkg CDN URL resolvability
Actual Result: unpkg resolves from npm; since shadcss is unpublished (404), the CDN URL cannot resolve
Root Cause Hypothesis: Same unpublished-package root cause
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Publish package, or replace CDN snippet with jsdelivr GitHub URL

Reproduction / Scenario: Serve root at apps/www (dev script) and request ../../ path
Expected Result: npx serve may block path traversal above served root
Actual Result: dev script serve . roots at apps/www; ../../ escapes the served root and most static servers 403/404 such traversal
Root Cause Hypothesis: Relative href points outside the served directory; serve sandboxes to its root
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Copy/symlink dist into apps/www and reference ./shadcss.min.css, or run the dev server from repo root

Reproduction / Scenario: serve roots at apps/www but CSS lives two levels up
Expected Result: All assets reachable from server root
Actual Result: CSS href ../../packages/... escapes serve root, breaking styling under the documented dev command
Root Cause Hypothesis: Server root and asset path mismatch
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Serve from repo root or vendor the CSS into apps/www

Reproduction / Scenario: Count inline JS handlers in showcase
Actual Result: 19 onclick, 5 showModal, 5 showPopover, 6 hidePopover, 1 oninput, 1 onerror present
Root Cause Hypothesis: Native dialog/popover/theme/slider need JS to trigger; claim ignores showcase glue code
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Reword claim to 0 JS in the framework CSS; showcase uses minimal inline triggers

Reproduction / Scenario: Auto-dark omits status tokens that explicit dark redefines
Expected Result: Auto dark and explicit dark yield identical palette
Actual Result: Explicit dark redefines --success/--warning/--info but the media-query block does NOT, so under OS-dark the success color stays its light value — inconsistent
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auto-dark block hand-maintained and drifted from explicit dark
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add --success/--warning/--info and -foreground to the prefers-color-scheme block

Reproduction / Scenario: Order survives import reordering by a bundler
Expected Result: An explicit @layer reset, theme, components; priming line guarantees order
Actual Result: No priming statement exists; order depends entirely on first-appearance, so reordering imports inverts precedence
Root Cause Hypothesis: Layer priority not declared up-front; fragile against tooling that changes source order
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add @layer reset, theme, components; as the first statement

Reproduction / Scenario: www dev fetches serve via npx at runtime
Expected Result: No unpinned remote code execution surprises
Actual Result: dev = npx -y serve . -l 3333. -y auto-installs latest serve unpinned; supply-chain exposure on each run, not added to devDependencies
Root Cause Hypothesis: Convenience over reproducibility; serve not pinned
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add serve to devDependencies with a pinned version and call via local bin

Reproduction / Scenario: packageManager=pnpm but only package-lock.json committed
Expected Result: pnpm-lock.yaml present if pnpm is the manager
Actual Result: package.json declares pnpm@9.0.0; repo root contains package-lock.json, no pnpm-lock.yaml. Corepack pnpm install would ignore package-lock.json and regenerate, drifting deps
Root Cause Hypothesis: Installed with npm while declaring pnpm; lockfile never migrated
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Pick one: commit pnpm-lock.yaml and delete package-lock.json, or set packageManager to npm version used

Reproduction / Scenario: Infinite animation runs continuously
Actual Result: No prefers-reduced-motion guard on shadcss-spin; animation runs unconditionally
Root Cause Hypothesis: Missing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){ .btn[aria-busy=true]::after{ animation: none } }

Reproduction / Scenario: Toggle changes state with zero JavaScript as framework implies
Expected Result: Clicking toggles pressed state without JS
Actual Result: CSS only styles [aria-pressed]; nothing flips the attribute, so a standalone .toggle needs JS to function
Root Cause Hypothesis: aria-pressed is not mutated by CSS; README 0 JS claim is contradicted for interactive toggle
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document that .toggle requires JS to flip aria-pressed, or recommend .toggle-group

Reproduction / Scenario: Label nested-inside-input or reordered markup breaks highlight
Actual Result: Highlight depends strictly on adjacent-sibling order; any wrapping label-around-input yields no checked styling
Root Cause Hypothesis: Adjacent-sibling combinator is order/structure-fragile and undocumented as a hard requirement
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document required input->label sibling order explicitly in registry markup/notes

Reproduction / Scenario: registry.json validates against cited shadcn schema
Actual Result: Uses non-conforming shape (components[] with file/deps/markup; shadcn uses items with type/files[].path/registryDependencies)
Root Cause Hypothesis: $schema points at shadcn but the document is a bespoke format
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Either remove the $schema line or publish/point to a custom schema matching this shape

Reproduction / Scenario: Fill does not track dragging without JS
Expected Result: Fill follows thumb as user drags
Actual Result: --slider-fill is static unless JS updates it; only the volume slider has oninput JS, the brightness slider has a hardcoded 75%
Root Cause Hypothesis: Pure CSS cannot read range value into a custom property
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document that fill requires JS, or ship a tiny oninput snippet

Reproduction / Scenario: Auto-advance to next box on digit entry
Expected Result: Typing a digit moves focus to next box
Actual Result: No JS present; with maxlength=1 focus does not advance, user must manually tab/click
Root Cause Hypothesis: OTP auto-advance is inherently JS; the file header admits pure-CSS not possible
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Ship optional JS for auto-advance, or document as visual-only

Reproduction / Scenario: Entry shape vs cited shadcn schema
Expected Result: Entries conform to shadcn schema (type, files[], registryDependencies)
Actual Result: Entries use non-shadcn fields: file (string), deps, classes, markup, attributes; no type/files[]/registryDependencies
Root Cause Hypothesis: Custom registry format reusing shadcn $schema URL
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Either point $schema at a custom schema or remap fields

Reproduction / Scenario: Header nav buttons actually change month
Actual Result: Nav buttons styled but have NO handler in showcase; file comment claims pure-CSS navigation but no mechanism exists
Root Cause Hypothesis: Calendar ships visual layer only; the No JS for navigation claim overstates
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove the No JS for navigation claim or ship the documented month-switch markup

Reproduction / Scenario: Grid exposes calendar/grid semantics to AT
Actual Result: Markup uses <button> day cells inside plain div; no role=grid, columnheader, or aria-label on grid; weekday letters are bare span
Root Cause Hypothesis: File comment claims accessible markup but no ARIA grid roles provided
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Recommend role=grid on .calendar-grid, role=columnheader on weekdays, aria-label dates

Reproduction / Scenario: Arrow buttons advance the carousel
Actual Result: CSS only styles .carousel-btn; advancing relies on inline onclick scrollBy, contradicting zero-JS
Root Cause Hypothesis: Arrow navigation not achievable in pure CSS; showcase uses inline onclick
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document arrows as requiring JS (or CSS ::scroll-button); do not present as zero-JS

Reproduction / Scenario: Typing filters/fuzzy-searches the list
Expected Result: Input narrows results, shows empty state when none
Actual Result: No filtering mechanism; .command-empty is static styling only; comment admits search needs JS
Root Cause Hypothesis: Fuzzy search not expressible in CSS; header references a <datalist> pattern absent from file
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Keep shell + JS framing; remove the unfulfilled <datalist> reference or provide that example

Reproduction / Scenario: Palette exposes combobox/listbox roles for AT
Actual Result: No ARIA roles; items are button/a with class only; selection uses data-selected not aria-selected
Root Cause Hypothesis: Selection state uses non-semantic data-selected; no combobox/listbox semantics
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Recommend role=dialog/listbox/option + aria-selected, aria-activedescendant wiring

Reproduction / Scenario: Screen reader announces tooltip text
Expected Result: Tooltip content exposed via role=tooltip / aria-describedby
Actual Result: Content is pure CSS generated content from attr(data-tooltip); no role, no aria-describedby; generated content inconsistently exposed by AT
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS ::after generated content is not a reliable accessibility tree node
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document requiring authors to also set aria-label/aria-describedby; cannot be fixed in CSS alone

Reproduction / Scenario: Touch device tap to open hover-card
Actual Result: @media (hover:none) sets display:none with no override; panel can never open on touch
Root Cause Hypothesis: Promised popover tap fallback was never implemented; only the hide rule exists
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add [popover] to .hover-card-panel and a :popover-open display:block rule, or remove the misleading comment

Reproduction / Scenario: Browser/UA where no hover media query matches
Actual Result: display:none only lives inside the hover-media block; outside it the panel has no display rule so it renders visible by default
Root Cause Hypothesis: Base hidden state is nested inside the hover feature query instead of being a default rule
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Move .hover-card-panel { display:none } to the top-level block, only flip to block on hover

Reproduction / Scenario: Keyboard/AT access to hover-card content
Actual Result: :focus-within reveal exists but only under pointer:fine; no ARIA relationship between trigger and panel
Root Cause Hypothesis: No ARIA wiring and reveal gated behind pointer:fine media query
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Provide :focus-within reveal outside the pointer:fine gate and document aria-describedby

Reproduction / Scenario: Document conforms to cited $schema
Actual Result: shadcn schema requires items with type/files[]/registryDependencies; this doc uses a bespoke components[] shape
Root Cause Hypothesis: $schema URL copied from shadcn but the document is a custom format
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Drop the $schema reference or point it at a self-authored schema

Reproduction / Scenario: Auto-dismiss with Popover API: claim of pure-CSS auto-dismiss for popover toasts is misleading
Expected Result: Popover toast auto-dismisses and is removed from top layer by CSS
Actual Result: CSS has zero :popover-open / @starting-style rules; after out-animation the popover is still open in top layer, only visually hidden; true removal needs JS hidePopover
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS cannot call hidePopover; popover lifecycle is JS-controlled
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document that popover toasts require JS hidePopover; reserve CSS auto-dismiss claim for in-flow toasts

Reproduction / Scenario: sonner-loading spinner depends on shadcss-spin keyframe not defined in sonner.css
Actual Result: sonner.css references shadcss-spin but does not define it; works only if another loaded component defines it. If imported standalone, spinner is static
Root Cause Hypothesis: Cross-file keyframe dependency not declared in registry deps
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Define shadcss-spin in sonner.css or add spinner to sonner deps

Reproduction / Scenario: Showcase applies popover to .sonner but CSS has no :popover-open
Actual Result: no :popover-open/@starting-style; demo stays open until manual hidePopover; the 4s out animation only fades visually
Root Cause Hypothesis: Popover lifecycle is JS-driven; CSS animation cannot pop the element
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document JS hidePopover requirement; do not claim pure-CSS dismiss for popover usage

Reproduction / Scenario: sonner-close button has no accessible label
Actual Result: CSS only; showcase close button contains svg-only, no aria-label
Root Cause Hypothesis: Icon-only button without text/aria-label
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add aria-label=Close to .sonner-close in markup/docs

Reproduction / Scenario: Required input WITHOUT a placeholder fails validation but shows no destructive border
Expected Result: Invalid empty required field shows destructive styling
Actual Result: :invalid:not(:placeholder-shown) cannot match an empty field that has no placeholder, so border stays neutral
Root Cause Hypothesis: Uses :placeholder-shown as a proxy for empty; without a placeholder the pseudo never applies
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add :user-invalid as an additional selector to cover placeholder-less fields

Reproduction / Scenario: Required text field WITHOUT placeholder, left empty and submitted
Expected Result: Empty required field shows its error message
Actual Result: :has(:invalid:not(:placeholder-shown)) cannot match an empty placeholder-less input; error stays hidden
Root Cause Hypothesis: Empty-state detection relies on :placeholder-shown; placeholder-less required text inputs never expose their error
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add .field:has(:user-invalid) .field-error { display:flex }

Reproduction / Scenario: Error message programmatic association
Expected Result: Error linked to control via aria-describedby / aria-invalid
Actual Result: CSS only toggles visibility; no aria wiring exists; screen readers will not announce the revealed error
Root Cause Hypothesis: Validation purely visual via :has(); no aria-invalid/aria-describedby relationship
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document required markup: add aria-describedby and aria-invalid (CSS cannot supply this)

Reproduction / Scenario: Document validates against its cited $schema
Expected Result: Shape conforms to shadcn registry schema
Actual Result: Cited schema expects items[].files[].{path,type}; this file uses components[] with file/deps/markup/classes and a tokens object — non-conforming
Root Cause Hypothesis: $schema URL copied from shadcn but the document uses a bespoke shape
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Either remove/replace the $schema or restructure entries

Reproduction / Scenario: Broken image URL
Expected Result: Component shows text fallback without JS
Actual Result: A failed img leaves a broken-image box; recovery requires inline onerror JS, contradicting zero-JS claim
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS cannot detect img load failure
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document onerror JS requirement, or render text under an absolutely-positioned img

Reproduction / Scenario: Spinner announced as busy/loading
Actual Result: No ARIA; showcase spinners are bare div with no label or role=status
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS-only component cannot add semantics; docs omit guidance
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add registry markup guidance recommending role=status aria-label=Loading

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry shape conforms to cited shadcn $schema
Actual Result: Items use custom {name,file,deps,markup,classes}; no type/files/registryDependencies
Root Cause Hypothesis: Reused shadcn schema URL while inventing a bespoke format
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Drop the $schema reference / point to a self-hosted schema, or restructure items

Reproduction / Scenario: Menu exposes ARIA menu/menuitem roles for screen readers
Expected Result: role=menu on container and role=menuitem on items
Actual Result: No role=menu or role=menuitem anywhere; items are bare a/button, container is plain div
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS-only component provides no ARIA roles and showcase omits them
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document required role=menu/role=menuitem in registry markup and showcase

Reproduction / Scenario: data-side anchoring positions menu relative to trigger
Expected Result: Menu anchors to its trigger on each side variant
Actual Result: data-side only sets inset/margin on the popover in top layer, not relative to trigger; without anchor positioning it offsets from viewport
Root Cause Hypothesis: Popover renders in top layer; inset values viewport-relative, so menu does not attach to trigger
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add anchor positioning with current inset rules as @supports fallback

Reproduction / Scenario: Menubar exposes role=menubar/menuitem and keyboard arrow navigation
Actual Result: No roles; left/right arrow navigation impossible without JS; only Tab works
Root Cause Hypothesis: Pure-CSS cannot implement roving tabindex/arrow nav
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document that menubar needs JS + ARIA; add roles to markup examples

Reproduction / Scenario: Nav exposes proper menu semantics / aria-expanded
Expected Result: Triggers convey expanded state, content has accessible name
Actual Result: Triggers are plain buttons; no aria-expanded/aria-controls beyond popovertarget; no role wiring
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS cannot toggle aria-expanded; showcase omits it
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document aria-expanded handling (needs JS) or rely on popovertarget UA semantics

Reproduction / Scenario: Right-click opens context menu at cursor in showcase
Expected Result: A .context-menu element is triggered via oncontextmenu somewhere in the showcase
Actual Result: index.html contains NO .context-menu markup and NO oncontextmenu handler; the component is unused/undemoed
Root Cause Hypothesis: Component is a styled shell whose required inline-JS trigger is never wired
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add a demo with oncontextmenu showPopover() plus cursor positioning, or mark JS-required in docs

Reproduction / Scenario: Checked items expose checkbox role/aria-checked
Actual Result: State is encoded only in data-checked for styling; no aria-checked/role provided
Root Cause Hypothesis: Visual-only checked state with no ARIA
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document required aria-checked + role=menuitemcheckbox alongside data-checked

Reproduction / Scenario: Menu items validate against cited shadcn $schema
Actual Result: context-menu/dropdown/menubar/navigation-menu items use non-conforming keys; no type or files[]
Root Cause Hypothesis: Registry advertises shadcn schema but uses a bespoke shape
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Point $schema at the actual custom schema, or migrate items to shadcn registry-item shape

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry has no standalone container entry
Expected Result: container component listed in registry.json components
Actual Result: grep finds container only in card description; no own component entry, so AI registry consumers cannot discover .container-sm/md/lg/xl/full
Root Cause Hypothesis: Component file shipped but omitted from registry components array
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add a container entry to registry.json components with classes container/-sm/-md/-lg/-xl/-full

Reproduction / Scenario: Vertical direction panel corner rounding
Expected Result: Rounded outer corners along vertical axis
Actual Result: first/last/only-child radii are hardcoded for horizontal layout only; vertical stacks get wrong rounding
Root Cause Hypothesis: Corner/border rules not branched on data-direction
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add [data-direction=vertical] overrides for first/last child radii

Reproduction / Scenario: Document validates against cited shadcn schema
Actual Result: Items use {name,file,deps,markup,classes} not shadcn {name,type,files,registryDependencies}; cited schema would reject
Root Cause Hypothesis: $schema borrowed from shadcn but custom shape adopted
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Point $schema to a custom schema or drop the field

Reproduction / Scenario: Screen reader announces tab semantics
Actual Result: No ARIA roles in CSS or showcase; only native radios/labels exposed
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS-only pattern cannot add roles; markup omits them
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add role=tablist/tab/tabpanel + aria-selected to documented markup

Reproduction / Scenario: Current page exposed to AT and nav landmark
Expected Result: aria-current=page on current and nav landmark
Actual Result: CSS uses class .breadcrumb-current not aria-current; showcase wraps in <nav> but lacks aria-current/aria-label
Root Cause Hypothesis: Styling keyed to class not ARIA; markup omits aria-current
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add aria-current=page to current item and aria-label to nav

Reproduction / Scenario: Disabled/active link removed from keyboard tab order
Expected Result: Cannot focus disabled prev or active link via keyboard
Actual Result: pointer-events:none blocks mouse only; <a href> remains focusable via Tab and aria-disabled does not remove from tab order
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS pointer-events does not affect keyboard; no tabindex=-1 in template
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document removing href or adding tabindex=-1 for disabled/active

Reproduction / Scenario: Collapse toggle works without JS
Expected Result: Sidebar collapses to icon width interactively
Actual Result: Collapse keyed to data-collapsed=true; nothing in CSS or showcase sets it, no toggle control. Requires external JS contradicting zero-JS framing
Root Cause Hypothesis: Stateful collapse needs JS; not provided
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document JS requirement; add a CSS-only :has(checkbox) toggle if zero-JS desired

Reproduction / Scenario: Mobile drawer slides in
Actual Result: Media query translates X -100% and reveals on data-mobile-open=true; but no JS sets it and backdrop needs data-mobile=true (never set), so drawer cannot open
Root Cause Hypothesis: Drawer open/backdrop driven by attrs only mutable via JS
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Provide a checkbox/:has() CSS toggle or document JS; fix backdrop attr

Reproduction / Scenario: Off-canvas drawer hidden from AT when closed
Expected Result: Closed drawer not reachable by keyboard/AT
Actual Result: transform:translateX(-100%) only visually hides; links remain focusable and exposed to AT; no inert/visibility handling
Root Cause Hypothesis: Transform does not remove from a11y tree or tab order
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add visibility:hidden when closed or document inert requirement

Reproduction / Scenario: Document validates against cited $schema
Actual Result: Uses custom shape instead of shadcn registry items[]/files[]; would fail schema validation
Root Cause Hypothesis: Non-conforming custom schema reusing shadcn $schema URL
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Point $schema to a custom schema or restructure

Reproduction / Scenario: .dialog-close styled but unwired and absent from showcase dialog
Expected Result: A visible top-right close button that dismisses the dialog
Actual Result: .dialog-close has only visual styles, no close behavior; the showcase dialog never uses .dialog-close. A consumer adding it gets a non-functional X without JS
Root Cause Hypothesis: Class provides styling only; closing requires JS or wrapping in <form method=dialog>
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Wrap the close glyph in <form method=dialog><button class=dialog-close> in the registry markup

Reproduction / Scenario: data-side positioning aligns popover to trigger
Expected Result: data-side moves the panel to the correct side of its trigger
Actual Result: data-side only flips margin direction; with no anchor positioning the panel is NOT positioned relative to the trigger. showcase uses data-side=bottom-start which is not even a defined variant
Root Cause Hypothesis: No CSS anchor positioning; data-side variants are margin nudges and don't include bottom-start
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Adopt CSS anchor positioning and define the bottom-start/bottom-end values

Reproduction / Scenario: Overlay entries validate against the cited shadcn schema
Expected Result: dialog/drawer/sheet/popover entries conform to shadcn registry-item shape
Actual Result: Entries use {name,file,deps,markup,classes,description} with no type or files[]; bespoke shape that does not conform
Root Cause Hypothesis: Authors reused shadcn $schema URL while inventing a custom shape
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Drop/replace $schema or restructure items to shadcn registry-item format

Reproduction / Scenario: Validate registry.json item shape against shadcn registry-item schema requirements
Expected Result: Each component item has type and a files array; cross-refs in registryDependencies
Actual Result: Items use custom keys file (string), deps, classes, markup, attributes; no type, no files[], no registryDependencies. Top level uses custom style/tokens/components
Root Cause Hypothesis: Author reused shadcn schema URL for credibility but invented a bespoke CSS-class registry format
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove the misleading $schema line, or point it at a custom/self-hosted JSON Schema

Reproduction / Scenario: A shadcn-aware validator/CLI fetches the cited schema and validates the document
Actual Result: Top-level tokens/style/components and item-level markup/classes/deps are not in the shadcn schema; required type missing on every item -> validation fails
Root Cause Hypothesis: Custom format mismatched with declared schema
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Drop the shadcn $schema reference; document the real format in README

Reproduction / Scenario: Documented components honestly flagged as needing JS
Actual Result: registry resizable (pair with 10 lines of JS) and AI_GUIDE list 6 JS cases; honest in body but headline badge still says JavaScript 0 lines
Root Cause Hypothesis: Body text honest; badges/headline overstate
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Change badge text to no-JS-framework or CSS-first; keep honest caveats

Reproduction / Scenario: shadcss JS=none row vs reality
Expected Result: Accurately reflects that opening dialogs/popovers/toasts needs JS
Actual Result: Table marks shadcss JS none while AI_GUIDE and the showcase 35 JS handlers show JS is required for core interactions
Root Cause Hypothesis: Marketing absolutism; JS none is true for the CSS bundle but false for interactive usage
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Footnote the JS column: no JS framework; dialogs/popovers need a one-line native call

Reproduction / Scenario: Indeterminate state is real (form/a11y) not just visual
Expected Result: Indeterminate exposed to AT and forms as mixed
Actual Result: Only a CSS attribute selector drives visuals; the real input.indeterminate IDL property is unset, so AT reports unchecked. Requires JS
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS cannot set the IDL indeterminate property; attribute-only is presentational
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document data-indeterminate is visual-only and requires el.indeterminate=true via JS

Reproduction / Scenario: Duplicate .radio-group definition across files
Actual Result: .radio-group defined twice: radio.css (flex) and radio-group.css (grid). In concatenated dist the later overrides to grid, making radio.css flex dead code
Root Cause Hypothesis: Two files own the same class with different layout models
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove the .radio-group block from radio.css and keep grid in radio-group.css

Reproduction / Scenario: data-orientation values disagree between the two .radio-group blocks
Expected Result: One consistent set of orientation values
Actual Result: radio.css overrides vertical; radio-group.css overrides horizontal. Grid base wins, so vertical override is moot
Root Cause Hypothesis: Divergent orientation contracts from the duplicate-class problem
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Consolidate orientation handling in radio-group.css supporting both

Reproduction / Scenario: Inline event handlers and CSP
Expected Result: No inline JS so a strict CSP works
Actual Result: Inline onclick/oninput/onerror present; strict script-src CSP would block all interactions
Root Cause Hypothesis: Inline handlers require unsafe-inline
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Externalize the few handlers into one tiny script file referenced via nonce/hash

Reproduction / Scenario: Open overlay with JS disabled
Actual Result: Only inline showModal triggers; no fallback
Root Cause Hypothesis: Native dialog open requires JS; CSS-only open not provided
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document JS requirement or use a checkbox/:target-based open pattern

Reproduction / Scenario: Stack multiple toasts
Actual Result: Each sonner hardcodes identical position: fixed; bottom right — they overlap, not stack
Root Cause Hypothesis: No stacking offset logic; needs JS or :nth offsets
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Use .sonner-region flow positioning, or JS to offset

Reproduction / Scenario: Resolve ./registry.json from apps/www
Actual Result: Not present in apps/www; only packages/shadcss/registry.json exists
Root Cause Hypothesis: Link assumes registry.json sits beside index.html
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Point href to ../../packages/shadcss/registry.json or copy the file into apps/www

Reproduction / Scenario: Resolve ./AI_GUIDE.md from apps/www
Actual Result: AI_GUIDE.md absent from apps/www
Root Cause Hypothesis: Dead relative link
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Correct the path to the actual AI_GUIDE.md location or add the file

Reproduction / Scenario: Mobile sidebar reachability
Expected Result: A trigger reveals nav on mobile
Actual Result: Sidebar set display:none under 1024px with no toggle to reopen it; mobile users lose nav and theme toggle
Root Cause Hypothesis: No mobile menu/sheet trigger despite a Sheet component existing
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Wire a hamburger that opens the existing sheet/drawer for mobile nav

Reproduction / Scenario: Tabs expose tablist/tab/tabpanel roles
Actual Result: No ARIA roles; radios+labels only, announced as radio group not tabs
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS-only tabs cannot get tab roles without JS/markup additions
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add role=tablist/tab/tabpanel and aria-selected

Reproduction / Scenario: Tooltip content in a11y tree
Actual Result: data-tooltip rendered via CSS ::after only; not in accessibility tree
Root Cause Hypothesis: Pseudo-element content is not announced by screen readers
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Mirror tooltip text into aria-label/aria-describedby

Reproduction / Scenario: Screen reader announces alert
Actual Result: CSS never sets role; registry markup omits role=alert. Showcase has zero role=alert
Root Cause Hypothesis: Component documented/used without ARIA role
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add role=alert to registry markup and showcase alert containers

Reproduction / Scenario: Reserved padding for close button
Expected Result: Right padding reserves space so text does not sit under the X
Actual Result: Padding rule requires .alert[role=alert].alert-dismissible, but showcase details has NO role=alert, so padding never applies and the close X can overlap content
Root Cause Hypothesis: Padding selector over-qualified with [role=alert]
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Drop [role=alert] from selector and/or add role=alert in markup

Reproduction / Scenario: AT announcement + dismiss control labeling
Expected Result: role=alert live region and labeled close control
Actual Result: No role=alert on the details; summary relies on showcase-supplied aria-label which CSS/registry do not mandate
Root Cause Hypothesis: ARIA not enforced by component contract
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document required role=alert and aria-label on summary

Reproduction / Scenario: Zero-JS claim vs opening the dialog
Expected Result: No JavaScript needed per framework tagline
Actual Result: Opening requires showModal(); showcase uses inline onclick=showModal(). README 0-JS claim contradicted for opening
Root Cause Hypothesis: Native <dialog> modal cannot be opened without JS
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document that opening alert-dialog requires showModal()/JS

Reproduction / Scenario: Validate file against cited $schema
Actual Result: shadcn schema expects items with type/files/registryDependencies; this file uses name/version/style/tokens/components — non-conforming
Root Cause Hypothesis: $schema points at shadcn but the structure is bespoke
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove/replace $schema or restructure to shadcn registry-item shape

Reproduction / Scenario: --z-modal and --z-popover collide at same stacking value
Actual Result: Both --z-modal and --z-popover = 1300, so popovers cannot reliably stack above modals
Root Cause Hypothesis: Copy duplication; popover should sit above modal or below overlay
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Set --z-popover to a distinct value per intended stacking

Reproduction / Scenario: Component imported standalone without reset/theme
Actual Result: button.css references var(--space-2) etc. which are undefined without tokens.css, leaving properties invalid/empty
Root Cause Hypothesis: Components hard-depend on tokens with no fallbacks
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Provide var() fallbacks or document tokens.css as a hard prerequisite

Reproduction / Scenario: readAndBundle reads source from disk but bundle() never uses it
Expected Result: Either source is fed to bundle, or the read is removed
Actual Result: Line 32 reads source into a var; bundle() re-reads from disk via filename and ignores source; build() destructures only {code}. Dead/duplicate disk read
Root Cause Hypothesis: Leftover from a transform-based approach; bundle() resolves @imports itself
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove the redundant fs.readFile and source return

Reproduction / Scenario: release uses npm publish but project declares pnpm packageManager
Expected Result: Publish tool consistent with declared package manager
Actual Result: release calls npm publish while packageManager=pnpm@9.0.0. Mixed toolchain
Root Cause Hypothesis: packageManager field copied in without aligning scripts/lockfile
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Align packageManager to npm or change scripts to pnpm and commit a pnpm-lock.yaml

Reproduction / Scenario: Duplicate workspace globs in package.json and pnpm-workspace.yaml
Actual Result: package.json workspaces and pnpm-workspace.yaml both list apps/*,packages/* — redundant; harmless only if kept in sync manually
Root Cause Hypothesis: Both managers configs left in tree
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Keep only the config for the chosen manager

Reproduction / Scenario: Package name shadcss available/published on npm
Actual Result: npm view shadcss version -> 404 Not Found (not published). release would publish a public unscoped name currently free; name-squat/typo risk until claimed
Root Cause Hypothesis: Never published; 0.1.0 only local
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Publish 0.1.0 to reserve the name, or scope it

Reproduction / Scenario: README 50 components vs actual file count
Actual Result: README and badge say 50 components; src/components has 52 files and shadcss.css has 52 component @imports. Off by 2
Root Cause Hypothesis: Components added after README count was written
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Update README/badge to 52 components or derive count from registry.json

Reproduction / Scenario: btn-link inside btn-group joins visually
Actual Result: btn-link sets height:auto/padding:0 conflicting with group's height/border-radius:0 rules; misaligns vertically
Root Cause Hypothesis: btn-link overrides height/padding that btn-group assumes uniform
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document btn-link as incompatible with btn-group, or scope link overrides with :not(.btn-group .btn)

Reproduction / Scenario: Disabled toggle state styling
Actual Result: No :disabled rule for .toggle; disabled toggle looks identical to enabled
Root Cause Hypothesis: Missing disabled styling for toggle
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add .toggle:disabled{ opacity:.5; pointer-events:none }

Reproduction / Scenario: Outline variant with 3+ items joins borders correctly
Expected Result: middle items collapse adjacent borders, square inner corners
Actual Result: Only :first-child and :last-child square corners; middle items retain full radius-md, producing rounded inner corners and double borders mid-row
Root Cause Hypothesis: Missing middle-item corner reset; first/last-only logic
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add :not(:first-child):not(:last-child){ border-radius:0 }

Reproduction / Scenario: Vertical + outline border collapsing
Actual Result: Outline border-collapse rules only target inline-start/horizontal corners; vertical outline groups get double horizontal borders and wrong rounded corners
Root Cause Hypothesis: Outline variant authored only for horizontal layout
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add vertical-specific border/corner rules under [data-orientation=vertical][data-variant=outline]

Reproduction / Scenario: Switch lacks role=switch (announced as checkbox not switch)
Actual Result: No role applied; native checkbox announces as checkbox not switch
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS-only library cannot add roles; comment claims a11y is free which overstates
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document adding role=switch in markup guidance

Reproduction / Scenario: sm/lg variants do not define RTL checked transform
Expected Result: RTL checked offset for sm/lg matches their widths
Actual Result: switch-sm/lg define only LTR :checked::before; the [dir=rtl] rule only targets base width, so sm/lg knobs overshoot/undershoot in RTL
Root Cause Hypothesis: RTL override not duplicated for size variants
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add [dir=rtl] rules for .switch-sm/.switch-lg checked::before

Reproduction / Scenario: Vertical slider via data-orientation
Actual Result: writing-mode vertical + fixed 8rem height; but fill gradient is still to right so in vertical mode the gradient axis no longer matches the visual track
Root Cause Hypothesis: Gradient direction not adapted for vertical orientation
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add a vertical override with linear-gradient(to top, ...)

Reproduction / Scenario: File comment proposes single-input letter-spacing approach but ships multi-input
Actual Result: Header describes a single-input striped-background fake, but the actual CSS + markup use multiple boxes
Root Cause Hypothesis: Stale design comment left in file
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove the misleading single-input comment block

Reproduction / Scenario: Separator and grouping semantics
Expected Result: OTP group is labeled/associated for SR
Actual Result: .otp-separator is a decorative span with no aria-hidden; group label not programmatically tied to inputs
Root Cause Hypothesis: No grouping semantics; separator may be read aloud
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add aria-hidden to separator and per-input aria-label or labeled group

Reproduction / Scenario: Determinate transition uses width on value pseudo
Actual Result: ::-webkit-progress-value transition:width slow; Firefox ::-moz-progress-bar has no width transition, so Firefox value jumps
Root Cause Hypothesis: transition declared only on WebKit value pseudo
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add transition: width to .progress::-moz-progress-bar

Reproduction / Scenario: Dots indicate/seek active slide
Expected Result: Active dot reflects current slide; clicking a dot seeks
Actual Result: Dots are static styled buttons; active state hardcoded via inline style; no scroll-tracking, no seek handler
Root Cause Hypothesis: Active-dot tracking and seek both require JS
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document dots as decorative/JS-driven; optionally use scroll-driven animations

Reproduction / Scenario: Reduced motion honored for smooth scrolling
Actual Result: scroll-behavior:smooth is unconditional; no reduced-motion guard
Root Cause Hypothesis: No @media (prefers-reduced-motion) override
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){ .carousel-viewport{ scroll-behavior:auto } }

Reproduction / Scenario: Very long tooltip text near viewport edge
Expected Result: Text wraps or repositions to stay visible
Actual Result: white-space:nowrap forces single line; transform centers at 50% with no collision logic, so it clips
Root Cause Hypothesis: Pure-CSS positioning cannot detect viewport collisions
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Offer a wrapping variant; acknowledge edge clipping as a platform limit

Reproduction / Scenario: File comment claims Popover API hover trigger
Expected Result: Implementation uses Popover API as documented
Actual Result: No popover attribute or ::backdrop; reveal is purely :hover/:focus-within + display toggle
Root Cause Hypothesis: Comment is aspirational/stale; actual mechanism is display toggle
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Correct the file header comment to describe the display-toggle mechanism

Reproduction / Scenario: Using .collapsible on non-details element
Actual Result: [open] attribute only exists on <details>; on a plain div the reveal/rotate never trigger, content stuck at 0fr (hidden)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Component hard-depends on native details semantics not enforced
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document that .collapsible MUST be a <details> element

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry markup omits .toast-region wrapper
Actual Result: registry markup is a bare .toast with popover, no .toast-region; region positioning thus unused
Root Cause Hypothesis: Markup sample drops the fixed region wrapper
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix:
Wrap markup in
...

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry lists sonner-region class but markup positions sonner via inline style instead
Actual Result: demo overrides with inline fixed position, bypassing region flex stacking
Root Cause Hypothesis: popover top-layer needs explicit position since region isn't the offset parent
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Use non-popover in-flow sonner inside .sonner-region for true stacking demo

Reproduction / Scenario: scroll-area-sm shrinks scrollbar
Actual Result: only ::-webkit width changes; Firefox scrollbar-width stays thin regardless of -sm/-lg
Root Cause Hypothesis: Firefox has no per-element scrollbar thickness control beyond thin/auto/none
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document that size variants are WebKit-only; acknowledge Firefox limitation

Reproduction / Scenario: Trailing-only icon input gets unwanted leading padding
Expected Result: With only a trailing icon, input keeps normal start padding and pads the end
Actual Result: .input-wrap .input unconditionally sets padding-inline-start:2.5rem; trailing padding requires .input-trailing
Root Cause Hypothesis: Leading padding applied to every .input inside a wrap regardless of icon position
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Scope leading padding to a leading-icon marker via :has()

Reproduction / Scenario: Demo bio textarea has no placeholder claims Max 280 chars but no maxlength and invalid never triggers
Actual Result: index.html textarea has no maxlength and no placeholder; :invalid:not(:placeholder-shown) cannot fire; the 280-char limit is description-only text
Root Cause Hypothesis: Invalid styling keyed on :placeholder-shown; demo control has neither placeholder nor maxlength
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add maxlength=280 to the demo textarea, or document the placeholder requirement

Reproduction / Scenario: form-field help-text turns red only when control non-empty
Actual Result: Gated on :placeholder-shown; an empty invalid control with no placeholder never reddens help-text
Root Cause Hypothesis: Same :placeholder-shown empty-proxy limitation as inputs
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add :user-invalid alternative selectors

Reproduction / Scenario: Stale comment asserting :user-invalid lacks support
Actual Result: field.css states :user-invalid lacks browser support; Baseline-supported since ~2023, so comment is outdated
Root Cause Hypothesis: Design decision based on obsolete compatibility info
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Update comment and adopt :user-invalid as primary trigger

Reproduction / Scenario: Image fallback when no img provided
Actual Result: No :has/:not selector exists; fallback is just text shown because there is no img element. The documented mechanism is absent
Root Cause Hypothesis: Stale comment describing a mechanism never implemented
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Edit the header comment OR add .avatar > img { position:absolute; inset:0; }

Reproduction / Scenario: Multiple skeleton-text lines; only last should be 60%
Actual Result: .skeleton-text:last-child{width:60%} fires for ANY last child, including a single-line block. Showcase overrides with inline width to fight it
Root Cause Hypothesis: Over-broad last-child rule assumes multi-line text groups
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Scope to .skeleton-text-group .skeleton-text:last-child{width:60%}

Reproduction / Scenario: Non-media child (text block) inside aspect-ratio
Actual Result: > * forces position:absolute, width/height 100%, object-fit:cover — text gets stretched/clipped. Misuse silently breaks
Root Cause Hypothesis: Universal child selector assumes replaced media
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Narrow selector to > img, > video, > picture, > iframe

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry classes list completeness vs CSS
Expected Result: Every class in registry exists in CSS
Actual Result: .dropdown is listed in registry classes but the CSS file defines no .dropdown rule; it is a layout wrapper only
Root Cause Hypothesis: Registry advertises a .dropdown class that carries no styles
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove dropdown from the classes array or add a documented .dropdown rule

Reproduction / Scenario: Showcase wrapper class matches registry/CSS
Expected Result: Item wrapper class used in showcase is defined/known
Actual Result: Showcase uses .menubar-menu-item wrapper but neither menubar.css nor registry defines or lists it; unstyled
Root Cause Hypothesis: Showcase invented an undocumented wrapper class
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add .menubar-menu-item to menubar.css and registry, or drop the wrapper

Reproduction / Scenario: Menu always opens bottom-start regardless of viewport edge
Expected Result: Menu repositions when near right/bottom edge
Actual Result: menubar-menu hard-codes inset bottom-start + margin-top with no data-side variants or collision handling
Root Cause Hypothesis: No anchor/collision logic
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add data-side variants or anchor positioning with try fallbacks

Reproduction / Scenario: 20rem min-width panel on narrow viewport
Actual Result: min-width:20rem fixed with no media query; on <320px viewports the panel overflows horizontally
Root Cause Hypothesis: Fixed min-width with no responsive cap
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Use min-width:min(20rem, calc(100vw - 2rem))

Reproduction / Scenario: Zero-JS claim vs documented inline JS requirement
Expected Result: Component works with no JavaScript per README 0-JS claim
Actual Result: context-menu.css header says it needs a tiny inline oncontextmenu to position — contradicts the zero-JS claim
Root Cause Hypothesis: Right-click + cursor positioning impossible in pure CSS
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Label context-menu as requires JS in README component table

Reproduction / Scenario: .prose kbd overrides component kbd, dropping box-shadow/min-width/height
Actual Result: Inside .prose, typography.css has equal specificity and no min-width/height/box-shadow, so prose kbds look different
Root Cause Hypothesis: Duplicate kbd ruleset in typography.css diverges from component
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove the .prose kbd block and rely on component kbd.css, or align declarations

Reproduction / Scenario: Missing data-direction attribute
Actual Result: No base grid-auto-flow without data-direction; children fall into a single auto row — unspecified/likely broken
Root Cause Hypothesis: Axis rules gated entirely behind attribute selectors
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add default grid-auto-flow: column; grid-auto-columns: 1fr; to base .resizable

Reproduction / Scenario: 0 lines of JS claim vs resizable
Actual Result: resizable.css and its registry entry require JS for resize, contradicting footer claim
Root Cause Hypothesis: Marketing claim overstated
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Qualify claim to core components ship 0 JS; resizable needs ~10 lines

Reproduction / Scenario: 13th tab (id suffix -13)
Expected Result: Panel shows when its radio checked
Actual Result: No rule for -13; panel stays display:none. Requires hand-written one-line rule per docs
Root Cause Hypothesis: Suffix enumeration is finite; -13+ unsupported out of the box
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Extend the suffix list to needed range or add a :target fallback

Reproduction / Scenario: Invalid CSS property focus-visible-anchor: contains
Actual Result: tabs-panel declares focus-visible-anchor: contains, which is not a recognized CSS property; ignored
Root Cause Hypothesis: Non-existent/typo property; no effect
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Remove line tabs.css:92

Reproduction / Scenario: Sheet rendered with no data-side
Actual Result: No default animation or inset; sheet centers via UA default with no slide. Registry markup shows data-side=right but it is not enforced
Root Cause Hypothesis: Base dialog.sheet omits a default-side fallback
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Apply the right rules to dialog.sheet:not([data-side])

Reproduction / Scenario: Drawer rendered with no data-side
Actual Result: Without data-side, no inset override is applied; base animation slides translateX(100%) but the panel is not pinned to the right edge, causing animation/placement mismatch
Root Cause Hypothesis: Right-edge pinning lives only under [data-side=right]
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Apply the right-side inset to dialog.drawer:not([data-side])

Reproduction / Scenario: Popover trigger/target ARIA association
Expected Result: Trigger exposes popup semantics and the panel has a role/labelledby
Actual Result: Popover API auto-adds implicit aria on the button, but the .popover panel has no role and title isn't programmatically linked
Root Cause Hypothesis: CSS-only library leaves ARIA wiring to consumers
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document adding role=dialog and aria-labelledby pointing at .popover-title

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry markup matches showcase usage for popover data-side
Expected Result: data-side values in markup match those styled in CSS
Actual Result: dropdown/popover markup and showcase use data-side=bottom-start/bottom-end, but popover.css defines only top/start/end; values diverge
Root Cause Hypothesis: Positioning vocabularies not reconciled between CSS and registry/showcase
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Define the bottom-start/bottom-end variants in popover.css or update registry/markup

Reproduction / Scenario: Search repo for any code that reads/parses registry.json
Expected Result: At least one consumer parses registry.json
Actual Result: Only README.md, packages README, AI_GUIDE.md, and index.html mention it — all docs/markup, no parser
Root Cause Hypothesis: registry.json is a hand-maintained doc artifact, not integrated with any CLI
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document registry.json as machine-readable reference only, not a shadcn-CLI registry

Reproduction / Scenario: registry.json drifts from source since nothing validates it
Expected Result: Tooling keeps registry in sync with src/components
Actual Result: No sync/validation step exists; counts already disagree (52 in registry vs 50 claimed elsewhere)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Manual maintenance with no CI check
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add a build-time assert comparing registry count to ls src/components/*.css and documented count

Reproduction / Scenario: Reconcile claimed 50 vs actual registry/file counts
Actual Result: registry.json has 52 items; src/components has 52 .css files; docs claim 50
Root Cause Hypothesis: Two components added without updating the 50 headline/inventory
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Update all 50 references and badges to 52, regenerate the AI_GUIDE inventory list

Reproduction / Scenario: AI_GUIDE inventory list completeness
Actual Result: AI_GUIDE lists names but count/content drift from registry 52 (naming differences, missing entries)
Root Cause Hypothesis: Hand-maintained list not regenerated
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Generate the inventory block programmatically from registry.json

Reproduction / Scenario: Two READMEs cite different gzip badge values
Actual Result: packages README badge says 15.2 KB; root README badge says 15.3 KB — minor inconsistency
Root Cause Hypothesis: Independent edits; bundle changed between badge updates
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Single-source the size; pick one value for both badges

Reproduction / Scenario: Version consistency between package.json and registry.json
Actual Result: package.json version 0.1.0 but registry.json version 0.2.0
Root Cause Hypothesis: registry.json bumped independently of package.json
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Single-source version or derive registry version from package.json at build

Reproduction / Scenario: Component count in comparison vs headline
Actual Result: Comparison says 50+ while headline says 50 and actual is 52
Root Cause Hypothesis: Inconsistent figures across sections
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Standardize to 52 everywhere

Reproduction / Scenario: Potential trademark/endorsement confusion with shadcn
Expected Result: Clear not affiliated with shadcn disclaimer given near-identical name and reuse of shadcn schema URL
Actual Result: No disclaimer of non-affiliation; name shadcss + shadcn keyword + ui.shadcn.com schema URL imply association; only clone of the aesthetic wording present
Root Cause Hypothesis: Heavy lean on shadcn identity for credibility without explicit non-affiliation notice
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add Not affiliated with or endorsed by shadcn/ui to README and remove the unused shadcn schema URL

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry deps completeness for checkbox
Expected Result: checkbox entry should list base/tokens dep
Actual Result: checkbox registry block omits a deps array entirely though CSS references tokens
Root Cause Hypothesis: Inconsistent registry authoring
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add deps: [base/tokens] to the checkbox registry entry

Reproduction / Scenario: Registry classes claim .radio-group belongs to radio component
Actual Result: radio registry lists classes [radio,radio-group] but radio-group is also a separate component owning the same class — overlapping ownership
Root Cause Hypothesis: Class ownership leaks across two registry components
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Drop radio-group from the radio entry classes

Reproduction / Scenario: Pre-:has() browser fallback for selected state
Actual Result: No @supports fallback; the :has(:checked) rule is dropped, leaving no visual selection; showcase radios lack .radio class
Root Cause Hypothesis: Reliance on :has() with no progressive-enhancement fallback
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add .radio class to inner inputs and/or an @supports fallback

Reproduction / Scenario: Persistence across reload
Actual Result: No localStorage write; reload resets to light
Root Cause Hypothesis: Handler only mutates DOM attribute
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Persist to localStorage and restore on load

Reproduction / Scenario: Toggle exposes pressed state
Actual Result: Plain button, no aria-pressed/role state
Root Cause Hypothesis: No ARIA wired to toggle state
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add aria-pressed and update it in the handler

Reproduction / Scenario: Count consistency across the page
Actual Result: Mix of 50+, 50, and a sidebar Components (50) that links only ~18 items
Root Cause Hypothesis: Sidebar curated to a subset while labels claim 50
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Align the count label with the actual listed/showcased components

Reproduction / Scenario: JS disabled and image fails
Actual Result: Without JS the onerror never runs; empty/broken avatar shows
Root Cause Hypothesis: Fallback is JS-only
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Provide a CSS ::after initial behind the image as a no-JS fallback

Reproduction / Scenario: Dropdown/menubar menu roles
Actual Result: Plain buttons + popovertarget; no menu roles or aria-expanded
Root Cause Hypothesis: Popover API gives no implicit menu semantics
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add role=menu/menuitem and aria-haspopup/expanded where feasible

Reproduction / Scenario: Calendar navigation buttons
Actual Result: Buttons are inert; no handler or CSS state to change months
Root Cause Hypothesis: Static markup, no JS bound
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Disable/aria-disabled the nav or note it is display-only

Reproduction / Scenario: Carousel dots indicate/control position
Actual Result: Dots are buttons with no handler; only first is hardcoded active
Root Cause Hypothesis: No JS to bind dots to scroll position
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Bind dots to scrollTo or present as static indicators

Reproduction / Scenario: OTP auto-advance between inputs
Actual Result: Plain inputs, no auto-advance; manual tabbing required
Root Cause Hypothesis: Auto-advance requires JS not present
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document as display-only or add minimal JS

Reproduction / Scenario: footer with data-justify=center
Actual Result: Only end and between are matched; center falls through to default flex-start, silently ignored
Root Cause Hypothesis: No rule for data-justify=center
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add .card-footer:where([data-justify=center]) { justify-content: center; }

Reproduction / Scenario: card-title conveys heading semantics
Expected Result: Title exposed as heading to AT
Actual Result: card-title is a styled div with font-weight 600; no heading role/element
Root Cause Hypothesis: Title is presentational only
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Document/encourage using <h3 class=card-title>

Reproduction / Scenario: Keyboard user focuses an interactive card
Actual Result: No :focus or :focus-visible rule; only :hover. Keyboard users get no lift, and the div is not focusable
Root Cause Hypothesis: Hover-only styling; no focus parity
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add .card-interactive:focus-visible mirroring hover

Reproduction / Scenario: Content taller than viewport
Actual Result: dialog has overflow:hidden with no max-height/scroll on body; tall content would be clipped
Root Cause Hypothesis: overflow:hidden plus no max-height clips overflowing body content
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add max-height (e.g. 90vh) and overflow:auto on .alert-dialog-body

Reproduction / Scenario: Focus trap and labeling
Expected Result: Modal traps focus and is labeled by title
Actual Result: Native showModal() provides focus trap; however no aria-labelledby links title and no role=alertdialog
Root Cause Hypothesis: Relies on native dialog semantics but omits role=alertdialog and aria-labelledby
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add role=alertdialog, aria-labelledby/aria-describedby

Reproduction / Scenario: data-orientation=diagonal (unsupported value)
Actual Result: Neither horizontal nor vertical rule matches; :not([data-orientation]) also won't match, so separator gets only base styles and collapses to invisible
Root Cause Hypothesis: Invalid orientation value matches no rule and skips the default branch
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Make default robust: apply horizontal sizing to base, override for vertical only

Reproduction / Scenario: Divider exposed to AT
Actual Result: Element is a styled div with no role=separator; registry markup uses <div> without role
Root Cause Hypothesis: No semantic role
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Recommend role=separator or <hr> in registry markup

Reproduction / Scenario: Alert classes completeness
Actual Result: Listed many but alert.css also defines alert-default which is omitted from registry
Root Cause Hypothesis: alert-default class exists in CSS but missing from registry
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add alert-default to the alert classes array

Reproduction / Scenario: alert-dialog markup reflects accessible usage
Actual Result: registry markup omits role=alertdialog and aria-labelledby, propagating the a11y gap
Root Cause Hypothesis: Example markup lacks ARIA wiring
Proposed Smallest-Safe Fix: Add role/aria attributes to the alert-dialog markup example
